name: IPEC Guidelines
description: Self-assessment against IPEC governance and risk expectations.

questions.text: Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?

guidance.title: Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?
guidance.risk: For governance-and-compliance-q1, this control improves auditability for insurance compliance and board-level financial governance accountability by proving that 'Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?' is governed, executed, and reviewable within Governance & Compliance. This includes demonstrable remediation governance, verifiable operating evidence, and traceable management accountability for governance-and-compliance-q1.
actions: Define and approve a targeted remediation plan for governance-and-compliance-q1 with named control ownership.
Implement and operationalize process/technical changes needed to satisfy 'Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?'.
Perform management review and control assurance testing for governance-and-compliance-q1, then formally close findings.
evidence: Approved procedure or standard mapped to governance-and-compliance-q1 and section 'Governance & Compliance'.
Dated operating records showing execution of 'Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?' over the latest review cycle.
Management or committee evidence confirming oversight decisions and remediation tracking for governance-and-compliance-q1.
Independent testing or QA validation confirming 'Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?' operates effectively and exceptions are remediated.

questions.recommendation: In Governance & Compliance, close gap governance-and-compliance-q1 by formalizing control execution for 'Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?'. Document accountable ownership, implementation milestones, and operating cadence aligned to insurance-sector governance, policyholder protection, and prudential reporting expectations from IPEC. Execute remediation in phases (design, deployment, and assurance review), and track milestones for governance-and-compliance-q1 in governance reporting until sustained effectiveness is demonstrated.
evidence_required: Approved procedure or standard mapped to governance-and-compliance-q1 and section 'Governance & Compliance'.
Dated operating records showing execution of 'Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?' over the latest review cycle.
Management or committee evidence confirming oversight decisions and remediation tracking for governance-and-compliance-q1.
Independent testing or QA validation confirming 'Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?' operates effectively and exceptions are remediated.
questions.compliance_relevance: For governance-and-compliance-q1, this control improves auditability for insurance compliance and board-level financial governance accountability by proving that 'Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?' is governed, executed, and reviewable within Governance & Compliance. This includes demonstrable remediation governance, verifiable operating evidence, and traceable management accountability for governance-and-compliance-q1.

questions.text: Is there a formally approved control effectiveness review cadence for Governance & Compliance, including escalation thresholds and accountable owners?
questions.recommendation: Establish a documented quarterly control-effectiveness review for Governance & Compliance with defined escalation criteria, assigned owners, and tracked remediation deadlines. Integrate outputs into executive risk reporting to maintain continuous assurance aligned with IPEC insurance governance, prudential conduct, and policyholder protection.
evidence_required: Approved review calendar and terms of reference for Governance & Compliance oversight activities.
Completed review packs showing KPI/KRI outcomes, exceptions, and root-cause analysis for governance-and-compliance-q2.
Risk committee or management minutes confirming escalation decisions and closure owners for Governance & Compliance findings.
Follow-up validation records proving corrective actions were implemented and re-tested within agreed timelines.
questions.compliance_relevance: Demonstrates sustained governance and audit-ready oversight for Governance & Compliance by evidencing periodic control evaluation, timely escalation, and accountable remediation under IPEC insurance governance, prudential conduct, and policyholder protection.

guidance.title: Is there a formally approved control effectiveness review cadence for Governance & Compliance, including escalation thresholds and accountable owners?
guidance.risk: Demonstrates sustained governance and audit-ready oversight for Governance & Compliance by evidencing periodic control evaluation, timely escalation, and accountable remediation under IPEC insurance governance, prudential conduct, and policyholder protection.
actions: Approve and publish a control review cadence and RACI model for Governance & Compliance.
Run periodic effectiveness reviews, capture findings, and assign remediation owners for governance-and-compliance-q2.
Report open exceptions to governance forums and verify closure through independent retesting.
evidence: Approved review calendar and terms of reference for Governance & Compliance oversight activities.
Completed review packs showing KPI/KRI outcomes, exceptions, and root-cause analysis for governance-and-compliance-q2.
